track: 3 — recall/discovery (Java-class IF-8: a JVM language with no package-cycle ban → IF-8 FIRES on a package cycle; the other four families a clean Shape-B)
shape: A+B — IF-8 FIRED (a 14-package strongly-connected cycle in the okhttp core module) + IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a silent. 1 genuine fire (GATED) + 45 named suppressions / 0 false defects.
engine_proxy: deterministic-detector-sweep (5 read-only agents via the generalized cross-language sweep harness; NOT a full Detect→Emit pipeline; no cost ledger). Build chain:  (5 agents, ~323k tokens). MAIN-LOOP VERIFICATION: the IF-8 14-package SCC + 17 mutual-import pairs were INDEPENDENTLY re-computed by the main loop (a separate Tarjan over its own grep of okhttp3 `import` edges) and agree EXACTLY with the agent (same 14-member SCC). All 5 agents finalized.

repo.name: square/okhttp
repo.stack: Kotlin (the OkHttp HTTP client) — the ELEVENTH instrumented stack and the FIRST Kotlin datapoint
repo.size: 96,535 Kotlin code LOC / 525 files; multi-module Gradle (okhttp core + okhttp-tls/brotli/dnsoverhttps/…)

analysis_scope: the okhttp core module okhttp3 package tree (okhttp/src/{jvmMain,commonJvmAndroid}/kotlin/okhttp3/**, 147 files / 39 packages); IF-8 over the package import graph (component = okhttp3 sub-package); IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a over the same core.
result_headline: Track-3 Kotlin field sweep over square/okhttp@eef98589: IF-8 FIRED on a 14-package strongly-connected import cycle anchored on the public okhttp3 package (Tarjan over 39 packages / 93 import-edges → one SCC of 14; 17 minimal 2-node mutual-import pairs; bidirectional 2-cycles verified on real file:lines, e.g. okhttp3 ↔ okhttp3.internal.connection). IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a stayed silent. 1 genuine fire (GATED) + 45 named suppressions / 0 false defects. Kotlin on the JVM is in the same structural class as Java for IF-8: kotlinc imposes NO package-cycle ban (Gradle bans only cross-MODULE cycles; this sweep scopes within the core module where package cycles are permitted).

fires.title: 14-package strongly-connected cycle within the okhttp core module, anchored on the public okhttp3 package
fires.why_core: the SCC includes the public-API package okhttp3 (OkHttpClient, Call, Request, Response, Interceptor) → High
fires.recorded_disposition: GATED / reported, not filed. A real, deterministic IF-8 fire (Kotlin permits package cycles). A KNOWN, accepted OkHttp design pattern (the okhttp3.internal.* layer implements the public okhttp3 types and the public types delegate to internal — a public↔internal mutual dependency); reported as a structural fact, deep tier, High because it touches the public Core package. Not a defect to fix.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: acyclic — no back-edge (verified: zero out-edges into the SCC for the sinks; graal feeds only platform)
intra-component recursion is not an SCC>=2 (same-package refs need no import; dropped at edge construction)

per_family.kotlin_specific_note: Kotlin on the JVM imposes NO package-cycle ban (like Java; unlike Go/Rust). kotlinc compiles intra-module package cycles; Gradle bans cross-MODULE cycles only, and the entire 14-package SCC lives within the single okhttp core module, so the cycle is real and compiles. The agent verified multiple INDEPENDENT bidirectional 2-cycles on real import file:lines (not a fragile single chain), and the main loop independently reproduced the 14-SCC / 17 pairs. Same structural class as Java (Signal-Server, 33-package SCC) and C#-namespace-level — permitted + explicit imports → groundable fire.

suppressed.category: enum class / sealed hierarchies / companion-const collections bucketed by canonical name across packages; same-named candidates are coincidental homonyms or same-component

per_family.kotlin_specific_note: Value-sets surface as enum class, sealed class/interface hierarchies, and companion-object consts. No cross-package canonical name carried a conflicting membership with behavioral entailment → 0 fires / 5 named suppressions.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: non-literal RHS / mixed condition / not a whole if-test — config is runtime

per_family.kotlin_specific_note: Kotlin has bool-only truthiness (like Java). `const val FLAG=true/false` is the only compile-time-const shape, and none gates a whole if-test as dead → 0 fires / 7 named suppressions.

suppressed.category: try/catch + runCatching across the networking core; every empty/typed catch either rethrows, closes-and-continues (non-empty), is a deliberate IOException expected-condition handler, is private, or is not cross-package-consumed

per_family.kotlin_specific_note: Kotlin error model = exceptions + nullable types + Result. okhttp (a networking lib) has dense IOException handling, overwhelmingly close-and-continue or rethrow (non-empty → §23 error-as-value suppress). 20 near-candidates, no bare empty cross-consumed catch → 0 fires.

suppressed.category: connection pools / dispatchers / caches (shared mutable, but accessed via typed APIs); @Volatile fields; companion vals
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: reached through a typed interface (mediated) / immutable val / per-instance

per_family.kotlin_specific_note: okhttp holds real shared mutable state (connection pool, dispatcher, caches) but accesses it through typed APIs (the explicit interface → mediated, suppress); no mutable companion/top-level `var` read/written by >=2 packages with no interface → 0 fires / 8 named suppressions.

key_kotlin_findings: IF-8 / no package-cycle ban → 14-package SCC FIRED: Kotlin on the JVM is in the same structural class as Java — kotlinc permits intra-module package cycles (Gradle bans only cross-module). The okhttp core is internally a tightly cyclic 14-package SCC anchored on the public okhttp3 package (public↔internal mutual dependency, a known OkHttp pattern), verified bidirectionally on real file:lines and double-verified (agent + main-loop Tarjan).

IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a clean: value-sets are coincidental homonyms; bool-only truthiness + runtime config starves IF-10; the networking error-handling is close-and-continue/rethrow (non-empty); shared connection/dispatcher state is typed-API-mediated.
honest_assessment: The ELEVENTH stack and first Kotlin datapoint, confirming Kotlin sits in the Java structural class for IF-8 (no package-cycle ban → a real 14-package SCC in the okhttp core, a known public↔internal layering pattern, recorded GATED). The other four families are a clean Shape-B with 45 mechanism-named suppressions — most tellingly IF-7c (20 networking catches, all non-empty/deliberate) and IF-3a (connection-pool/dispatcher state, all typed-API-mediated). 1 genuine GATED fire + 0 false defects + 45 named suppressions.

totals.counts_note: IF-8 fire double-verified (agent + main-loop Tarjan, same 14-SCC / 17 pairs).
